title: rubus angulatus
intro: Rubus angulatus, also known as the thimbleberry, is a type of bramble or thorny shrub belonging to the rose family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in its root and leaves, which have been found to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Rubus angulatus include its ability to reduce swelling and pain, as well as its potential to aid in the treatment of digestive issues and respiratory problems. In herbal preparations, the roots and leaves of Rubus angulatus are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be taken internally to leverage its medicinal properties.
